12.headline: Week 12 Recap: Pooh Pounces While the Coyotes Howl
12.intro: Week 12 delivered a pair of lopsided beatdowns at the top of the standings and a couple of grind-it-out category battles below them. The contenders separated themselves while the cellar grew a little darker.

matchups.narrative: The Pirates rode elite pitching to a comfortable 9-3 win, taking nine of twelve categories. David Glancy's staff posted a 3.64 ERA and a sparkling 1.088 WHIP, while the offense hit .302 with 52 runs to bury Chicago across the board. The Orphans claimed SB, QS, and HD, but were outclassed everywhere it mattered. Patrick Booth's club drops to 60-66 with the loss.
matchups.day_by_day: Chicago jumped out with a 6-run, 22-strikeout opener on 06-15, but cratered on 06-18 with a brutal -11 run swing. Pittsburgh built steadily, then sealed it with a 7-run, 3-homer eruption on 06-20 before both teams limped through the final day.
matchups.standout_players: Joey Cantillo was masterful for the Pirates with 8.0 IP, 9 K, and a 1.13 ERA, backed by Bryan Reynolds (2/5, HR, 3 RBI, 2 R). Bryce Harper led the Orphans with a 3/4, HR, RBI day, and MacKenzie Gore fanned 10 over 7 innings in a losing effort.

matchups.narrative: Phoenix steamrolled All Betts Are Off 10-2, dominating ten categories behind a monster 7 quality starts and 83 strikeouts. The Coyotes' .894 OPS and 17 home runs simply overwhelmed Andrew Powell's lineup. All Betts salvaged R and SB, but that was the extent of the resistance. Powell's squad falls to a league-worst 26-99.
matchups.day_by_day: Phoenix grabbed the early edge with a 4-run, 2-homer 06-15, while All Betts kept clawing back in runs throughout. The pitching gap proved decisive — Phoenix piled up 15 strikeouts on 06-19 alone, and the homer category was never close.
matchups.standout_players: Jac Caglianone went off for Phoenix with 2 HR, 3 RBI, and 3 R, while Cam Schlittler struck out 13 over 6 scoreless. Ivan Herrera (3/5, HR, 3 RBI, 3 R) and William Contreras (4/5, HR, 3 RBI) gave All Betts some pop, and Paul Skenes turned in a quality 6 IP, 8 K, 3.00 ERA.

matchups.narrative: Kevin Glancy's Pooh's On First put on a clinic, sweeping ten of eleven contested categories in an 11-1 rout — and the lone loss was simply a strategic punt on saves. The pitching was suffocating at a 2.96 ERA and 1.14 WHIP across nine quality starts, while the bats hit .270 with a .832 OPS. It was the most complete performance of the week, and it pushed Pooh's into the weekly roto crown. A statement showing from a team built to contend.
matchups.day_by_day: Lets Go Bucs actually opened strong with 6 runs and 3 homers on 06-15, but Pooh's answered with relentless quality starts and never let the Bucs back into the category fight. By midweek the rotation had quietly locked down ERA, WHIP, and QS, and the result was academic.
matchups.standout_players: Bobby Witt Jr (2/2, HR, 2 RBI, 2 R) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (2/4, HR, 2 RBI) led a balanced attack, while Nolan McLean fired 7 scoreless with 9 K. Dominic Canzone (HR, 2 RBI) and Parker Messick (6 IP, 9 K) were the bright spots for Sherri Glancy's Bucs.

matchups.narrative: The Big Gamblino took care of business 9-3, winning nine categories — as the league leader presumably should. Ted Zhang's bats clubbed a week-best 20 home runs and 53 RBI, and the team led the league in both, which is the kind of thing that happens when you've got Schwarber and friends. That said, the 4.85 ERA and 1.365 WHIP were hardly intimidating, and the Piglets did manage to snag SB, QS, and HD. A win is a win, even if the ratios weren't pretty.
matchups.day_by_day: The Piglets actually outscored Gamblino 9-6 with 5 homers on opening day 06-15, but couldn't sustain it. Gamblino's 06-20 power surge — 4 runs and 5 home runs — flipped the momentum for good as Pittsburgh's offense went quiet down the stretch.
matchups.standout_players: JJ Wetherholt powered Gamblino with a 3/5, 2 HR, 3 RBI, 3 R line, joined by Kazuma Okamoto and Kyle Schwarber (each 1 HR, 3 RBI). Jesus Luzardo added 7 IP, 9 K, 2.57 ERA. Nick Kurtz (HR, 3 RBI) and Eduardo Rodriguez (7 IP, 1.29 ERA) led the Piglets.

matchups.narrative: Cronen-Zone edged Seattle 7-4 in the week's most competitive matchup, leaning on power and pristine ratios. Andrew Stephens' squad smacked 18 homers with a 2.68 ERA and 1.19 WHIP despite a feeble .210 average. Seattle countered with strikeouts (95, a league high), quality starts, and steals, but the power gap and bullpen edge tipped the scales. The two sides split a tie in holds.
matchups.day_by_day: Seattle stayed close through a wild week of swings, including a 7-run 06-17 that kept things tight. The decider came on 06-21, when Cronen-Zone exploded for 9 runs and 4 homers to close out a back-and-forth battle.
matchups.standout_players: Pete Alonso (2/3, HR, 3 RBI, 2 R) and TJ Rumfield (HR, 3 RBI) carried Cronen-Zone's power, with Ranger Suarez tossing 6.2 scoreless. Logan Gilbert was excellent for Seattle (6.1 IP, 8 K, 1.42 ERA), but the Dumpers' bats managed just 5 homers all week.

12.roto_section: Pooh's On First seized the weekly roto crown with 118.5 points, fueled by top marks in RBI, SB, AVG, K, QS, ERA, and WHIP. Phoenix (106.0) and Pittsburgh Pirates (105.5) battled close behind, while The Big Gamblino (101.0) led the power categories with 20 HR and 53 RBI. At the bottom, All Betts Are Off limped to 63.0 points, salvaging only steals.
12.standings_note: The Big Gamblino remains atop the season standings at 83-41-8, but Phoenix (78-47) and a surging Pooh's On First (74-50) are keeping the pressure on. The gap at the bottom is widening, with All Betts Are Off mired at 26-99.

13.headline: Week 13 Recap: Dumpers Surge While the Cellar Sinks Deeper
13.intro: Week 13 saw blowouts pile up, with five of six matchups decided by six categories or more. Seattle topped the weekly roto chart while the bottom of the league continued its slow-motion freefall.

matchups.narrative: Pittsburgh Pirates steamrolled the Coyotes 10-2, sweeping nearly every offensive category behind a .288 average and a 12-homer week. Phoenix's bats cratered to a .201 mark and a .588 OPS, salvaging only ERA (3.25) and Holds (6). The Pirates' arms racked up 98 strikeouts and 10 saves, leaving little for the Coyotes to grab. It was a tough draw for Phoenix, whose pitching ratios were actually fine — the offense simply never showed up.
matchups.day_by_day: The Pirates jumped out early with a 6-run, 2-homer Monday before the Coyotes briefly punched back with a 6-run, 3-homer Tuesday. From there Pittsburgh's depth wore Phoenix down, particularly across the strikeout battles midweek. The categories were effectively locked well before the weekend.
matchups.standout_players: Isaac Paredes led Phoenix with a 3-for-5, homer, 2-RBI, 2-run effort in a losing cause. For the Pirates, Manny Machado homered and Joey Cantillo dazzled with 6 innings of 9-strikeout, 1.5-ERA ball, while Taj Bradley added 7 frames of 2.57 ERA work.

matchups.narrative: The Chicago Orphans edged Lets Go Bucs 6-4 in the week's tightest battle, with two categories ending in ties. Chicago won the power and average departments — 12 homers, 45 RBI, a .280 mark — while Lets Go Bucs countered with elite pitching, taking K, QS, ERA (2.74) and WHIP (1.037). Runs and Saves both finished deadlocked. In the end, the Orphans' offensive edge proved just enough.
matchups.day_by_day: Lets Go Bucs erupted for 6 runs and 35 strikeouts on Tuesday to seize early momentum before fading midweek. Both teams unloaded on Saturday — Lets Go Bucs put up 10 runs and 3 homers, Chicago answered with 6 runs and 3 homers. A quiet Sunday for both sealed the narrow margin.
matchups.standout_players: Christian Walker (1-for-4, HR, 3 RBI) and Ketel Marte (1-for-3, HR) paced the Bucs, with Parker Messick striking out 10 over 7.2 innings. Michael Wacha was Chicago's anchor with 7.2 frames of 1.17 ERA ball, while Julio Rodriguez chipped in a 2-for-4 with a steal.

matchups.narrative: Pittsburgh Piglets cruised past All Betts Are Off 9-3, leading the entire league with 48 runs and 14 home runs. The Piglets dominated the counting stats and the pitching ledger alike, taking K, QS, SV, HD and ERA. All Betts Are Off held serve only in the ratio categories — AVG, OPS and WHIP. It was another rough week for the league's last-place outfit.
matchups.day_by_day: The Piglets opened hot with a 4-run, 3-homer Monday and an 8-run Tuesday to build a commanding lead. All Betts Are Off hung around through midweek but a brutal 9-run, 4-homer collapse in the negatives on Thursday ended any hope. The Piglets coasted from there.
matchups.standout_players: Miguel Vargas went a perfect 2-for-2 with a homer and 2 RBI, while Shohei Ohtani logged a quality start with 8 strikeouts at a 3.00 ERA. Trevor Rogers was the lone bright spot for All Betts Are Off, spinning 6.1 innings of 1.42 ERA ball.

matchups.narrative: Seattle Dumpers handled Pooh's On First 8-3, riding a league-best .879 OPS and a sparkling 2.73 ERA. Pooh's On First actually matched Seattle in home runs (13 apiece) and won RBI, SB and strikeouts, but their pitching ratios betrayed them with a 4.67 ERA and 1.388 WHIP. Kevin Glancy's squad showed real punch at the plate — 45 RBI and a league-leading 9 steals — but simply ran into Seattle's buzzsaw of an arm staff. A frustrating result given how loud the bats were.
matchups.day_by_day: Seattle built early leads with strong Monday and Tuesday outputs, including 4 runs and 2 homers on Tuesday. Pooh's On First clawed back with a 3-homer Thursday but couldn't overcome the ratio gaps. Both offenses sputtered into the weekend, leaving Seattle's cushion intact.
matchups.standout_players: Luis Garcia Jr. carried Seattle with a monster 3-for-5, 2-homer, 5-RBI, 3-run day, and Foster Griffin tossed 7 shutout innings with 9 Ks. Junior Caminero was terrific for Pooh's On First, going 3-for-3 with a homer and 2 RBI, while Braxton Ashcraft fanned 10 over 6 innings at a 1.5 ERA in an otherwise leaky staff effort.

matchups.narrative: The Big Gamblino rolled to a 9-2 win, which — well, obviously — is what the league's top seed is supposed to do against last-place-adjacent competition. Ted Zhang's club piled up 44 runs, 10 homers and 6 saves, and the only cracks were the categories they shrugged off, dropping AVG and WHIP (a not-great 1.346) to Cronen-Zone. The win came largely on the strength of a single explosive Sunday rather than week-long dominance. Solid result, even if the ratios suggest the floor was lower than the scoreline implies.
matchups.day_by_day: Gamblino jumped out with a 9-run Monday, then went quiet for most of the week, even slipping into negatives on multiple days. Cronen-Zone hung tough through midweek but never threatened. A massive 9-run, 3-homer, 21-strikeout Sunday let Gamblino slam the door.
matchups.standout_players: Ryan O'Hearn powered Gamblino with a 3-for-5, 2-homer, 4-RBI, 3-run line, backed by Chris Sale's 10-strikeout, 1.5-ERA gem. Pete Alonso (1-for-4, HR, 2 RBI) and Nathan Eovaldi (7 shutout innings, 9 Ks) were the only real positives for Cronen-Zone.

13.roto_section: Seattle Dumpers topped the weekly roto board with 116.5 points, leading in R, HR, AVG, OPS, HD, ERA and WHIP, just ahead of Pittsburgh Pirates at 110.0. The Pittsburgh Piglets paced the league in both runs (48) and homers (14), while Pooh's On First led RBI (45) and steals (9). At the bottom, Cronen-Zone managed just 48.5 points with no category leads, and All Betts Are Off (70.5) likewise failed to top a single stat.
13.standings_note: The Big Gamblino remains comfortably atop the season standings at 92-43-9, with Seattle, Phoenix and the Piglets bunched tightly behind in the .57-.59 range. All Betts Are Off continues to sink at 29-108-7, now firmly anchored in the basement.

14.headline: Week 14 Recap: Pirates Plunder, Piglets Squeal, and a Bear Awakens
14.intro: Week 14 belonged to the raiders — Pittsburgh's big club ransacked the categories while a certain bear-themed roster stomped its way to a double-digit rout. Elsewhere, an exhausted 6-6 stalemate proved not every clash gets a clean ending.

matchups.narrative: The Pittsburgh Pirates simply overwhelmed Lets Go Bucs, taking nine categories to two behind a monster offensive line — 58 runs, 23 homers, 64 RBI and a .291/.938 slash. Lets Go Bucs salvaged pride in SB and QS, and their rotation actually held serve with five quality starts, but they were buried in the power and ratio categories. A 4.16 ERA and 1.40 WHIP couldn't stand up to Pittsburgh's 3.30/1.07. The gap was as wide as it looks.
matchups.day_by_day: The two sides traded blows early, splitting the opening days before Pittsburgh detonated on July 2 with a 13-run, five-homer barrage. Lets Go Bucs fired back on the Fourth with six runs and three bombs, but Pittsburgh slammed the door with a 10-run July 5 to close it out.
matchups.standout_players: Riley Greene led Pittsburgh's charge going 3-for-4 with a homer and 4 RBI, with Otto Lopez adding a 3-for-4, 3-RBI day. Sean Burke was dominant with 11 K over 6 innings at a 1.50 ERA. For the Bucs, Justin Wrobleski's 7 IP, 11-K gem was a bright spot in a lopsided week.

matchups.narrative: Phoenix Coyotes rode elite pitching to a 9-3 win, posting a 3.39 ERA and 1.06 WHIP while racking up 96 strikeouts. The Pittsburgh Piglets countered with more runs (41 to 33), a stolen base edge, and a QS advantage, but their .243 average and 0 saves left too many boxes empty. The Coyotes' arms and power (12 HR to 9) did the heavy lifting.
matchups.day_by_day: The Piglets swung the middle of the week with an 8-run July 1, but the Coyotes owned the strikeout column with a stunning 29-K July 30 surge. Phoenix sealed it with a six-homer explosion on July 5 that no comeback could match.
matchups.standout_players: Drake Baldwin powered the Coyotes with a 2-for-4, 1 HR, 4-RBI night, and Cristopher Sanchez tossed 7 shutout innings with 9 K. Shohei Ohtani did his two-way thing for the Piglets — a hit and RBI at the plate plus 6 IP, 9 K on the mound — but it wasn't enough support.

matchups.narrative: Seattle Dumpers delivered a pitching clinic, riding a microscopic 1.78 ERA and 0.95 WHIP to a 9-3 dismantling of The Chicago Orphans. Eight quality starts and five holds gave Seattle the pitching sweep outside of saves. Chicago hit for a better average (.292 to .272) and edged OPS and saves, but Seattle's power and run production (49 R, 14 HR) proved decisive.
matchups.day_by_day: Seattle jumped ahead early and never trailed, quietly building leads through the first three days. Chicago's July 1 rotation cratered with 32 strikeouts allowed, and though they clawed with a six-run July 3, Seattle's ratios were untouchable.
matchups.standout_players: Yoshinobu Yamamoto, Bryce Miller, and Logan Gilbert combined for a trio of shutout quality starts that anchored Seattle's dominance. Bo Bichette chipped in 3-for-5 with 3 RBI. Jo Adell's 3-for-4 led Chicago's bats, and Troy Melton's scoreless 6.1 innings deserved a better outcome.

matchups.narrative: A true stalemate: All Betts Are Off and Cronen-Zone split it 6-6. All Betts mashed their way to the offensive categories — 47 runs, 17 homers, a .291 average and .879 OPS — but a ghastly 7.04 ERA and 1.59 WHIP handed the pitching split to Cronen-Zone. Cronen-Zone took strikeouts, quality starts, holds, and both ratio categories to force the tie. Neither side could land the knockout.
matchups.day_by_day: Cronen-Zone opened strong with a 19-K July 29, but All Betts answered with a three-homer July 30. The pivotal July 2 saw All Betts drop 8 runs and 5 homers off their ledger, letting Cronen-Zone crawl back and grind out the deadlock.
matchups.standout_players: Rafael Devers went yard twice (2-for-5, 2 HR) for All Betts, with Aaron Nola's 7-inning, 7-K quality start propping up the arms. Zach Neto's 3-for-5, 1 HR, 2-run effort and Shane Baz's 2.57-ERA quality start kept Cronen-Zone level.

matchups.narrative: Pooh's On First turned in a signature week, thumping league-leading The Big Gamblino 10-2 and grabbing ten of twelve categories. The bear crew led the entire league in stolen bases (17), average (.303), and strikeouts (100), pairing speed and contact with a tidy .917 OPS. The Big Gamblino, so often the class of the league, managed only RBI and saves — a rare and quietly satisfying stumble for the season's runaway leader. When Pooh's bats and arms click like this, it's genuinely fun to watch.
matchups.day_by_day: Pooh's set the tone immediately with an 8-run, 17-strikeout July 29 and stacked four homers on July 30. Gamblino kept things respectable on the Fourth with a two-homer day, but never seriously threatened the categorical avalanche already in motion.
matchups.standout_players: Jordan Walker crushed a homer and drove in 4 for Pooh's, while Dylan Cease spun 7 shutout innings with 9 K and Sandy Alcantara went 8 strong at a 1.13 ERA. Willson Contreras (1-for-4, HR, 3 RBI) was about all Gamblino could muster — Joe Ryan's shutout start went to waste.

14.roto_section: Pittsburgh Pirates ran away with the weekly roto crown at 120.5 points, topping a staggering nine categories including R (58), HR (23), RBI (64), and SV (7). Pooh's On First (114.5) and Seattle Dumpers (113.5) gave chase — Pooh's led the league in SB (17), AVG (.303), and K (100) while Seattle owned the ratios with a 1.78 ERA and 0.95 WHIP. At the bottom, Cronen-Zone (69.0) failed to lead a single category, edging out All Betts Are Off (72.0) for the weekly cellar.
14.standings_note: The Big Gamblino (94-53-9) still commands the season standings despite the Week 14 setback, with Seattle, Phoenix, and Pooh's On First bunched right behind. All Betts Are Off remains mired at 35-114-7, and even their competitive 6-6 tie barely moves the needle in the basement.
